title: (No AI) Alexa is moving into Amazon.com
author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Screenshot-2026-05-13-at-7.44.36AM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Alexa for Shopping is Amazon’s new AI-powered shopping assistant. | Image: Amazon

Amazon is bringing Alexa Plus to [Amazon.com](http://amazon.com), integrating its [LLM-powered AI assistant](https://www.theverge.com/report/787171/amazon-alexa-plus-hardware-event-smart-home) directly into the company's shopping experience.

[Beginning today](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/retail/alexa-for-shopping-ai-assistant), when you type a query into Amazon, you'll be talking to Alexa for Shopping, the company's new shopping assistant, powered by Alexa Plus. So, while a search for "toilet paper" will still return the expected list of brands, typing "What's a good skincare routine for men" or "When did I last order AA batteries" will now trigger an answer from Alexa.

Alexa for Shopping is replacing [Amazon's Rufus AI shopping assistant](https://www.theverge.com/2024/7/12/24197173/amazon-ai-shopping-assistant-rufus-us-customers-rollout) and, unlike Rufus, it will be front and center in the Amazon app and on the …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/929457/amazon-announces-alexa-for-shopping-ai-assistant-rufus)

Amazon is bringing Alexa Plus to Amazon.com, integrating its LLM-powered AI assistant directly into the company's shopping experience. 
Beginning today, when you type a query into Amazon, you'll be talking to Alexa for Shopping, the company's new shopping assistant, powered by Alexa Plus. So, while a search for "toilet paper" will still return the expected list of brands, typing "What's a good skincare routine for men" or "When did I last order AA batteries" will now trigger an answer from Alexa.
Alexa for Shopping is replacing Amazon's Rufus AI shopping assistant and, unlike Rufus, it will be front and center in the Amazon app and on the  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Data centers are coming for rural America
author: Abigail Bassett
content:
![Photo collage of a data center with data visualizations.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STKS528_DATA_CENTERS_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

At its peak, the Androscoggin paper mill in Jay, Maine, a rural town about 67 miles northwest of Portland, employed about 1,500 people - until a pulp digester exploded in 2020, forcing the mill to close permanently.

In 2023, the 1.4 million-square-foot facility was [purchased through a joint venture by JGT2 Redevelopment](https://boulos.com/androscoggin-mill-in-jay-maine-undergoes-transformation/) and a number of other holding and capital companies. The project is led by developer Tony McDonald. Over the next three years, McDonald and his team broke down the mill's machinery and shipped it to Pakistan, and worked to clean up the industrial site for resale. That resale agreement was finalized earlier this year, accord …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/928963/data-center-rural-america-jobs-jay-maine)

That resale agreement was finalized earlier this year, accord …

creator: Lily Hay Newman
title: (No AI) WhatsApp Adds Meta AI Chats That Are Built to Be Fully Private
